scope: Three F1 framings (A/B/C) for the N-substrate composite + recommended choice + rationale.

F1_A_substrate_axis_framing.summary: AKIDA = independent axis. Composite = sum/weighted-average over 3 substrates (CLM x EEG x AKIDA) + 21 N-axes parallel.
pros: Symmetric — every substrate is a peer
Aligns with F1 verdict.json axis_weight_matrix already in use
cons: Ignores cross-substrate binding (the actual physics question)
Weights AKIDA pre-arrival = 0 — AKIDA arrival event creates a step-discontinuity in score
Tension_link cannot be cleanly placed (mediator vs axis)

F1_B_mediator_framing.summary: tension_link = bridge between CLM and EEG. AKIDA = future addition. Focus = bridge bandwidth / fidelity / directional flow.
F1_B_mediator_framing.rationale_zero_now: N-1 BRIDGE 3/4 PASS but with synth-CLM hybrid; no live concurrent CLM+EEG measurement yet. Mediator framing demands actual bandwidth measurement, which is not yet executed.
pros: Aligned with the actual cross-substrate binding question (Crick-Koch binding-by-synchrony)
Cogitate 2025 adversarial test FAIL on long-stim persistence motivates cross-substrate framing
cons: Ignores 21 N-axes that are NOT mediator-style (N-9 nexus, N-21 IIT reproductions, etc.)
Strict 3-axis (CLM x tension x EEG) drops AKIDA entirely

F1_C_hybrid_framing.summary: Independent axes (substrate-class witnesses) + Mediators (cross-substrate binding axes). Composite = independent + mediator binding contribution.

mediators.couples: CLM x EEG x AKIDA Landauer
CLM x EEG x AKIDA GWT broadcast

pros: Captures both substrate-witness AND cross-substrate binding (the two distinct epistemic questions)
Naturally accommodates AKIDA as both substrate AND mediator-anchor for 4 N-axes
Tension_link gets a defensible role (CLM<->EEG mediator) without overclaiming substrate-class
Allows post-AKIDA cascade to add 6 more axes coherently (N-2/3/4/5/7/8)
cons: More moving parts; weight assignments require honest C3
Mediator weights (0.04-0.05) are spec-estimates not pre-registered
Risk of double-counting if a mediator score is added to both endpoints

recommendation.rationale_one_line: Hybrid framing is the only one that simultaneously honors substrate-as-witness epistemics (own#2(b)) AND cross-substrate binding (Crick-Koch / Cogitate-2025 binding question), without forcing AKIDA-arrival step-discontinuity or losing 21 non-mediator N-axes.
recommendation.verdict_band_now: RED (F2 override active; binding hypothesis unverified)
recommendation.verdict_band_post_AKIDA_optimistic: YELLOW if at least 2 of N-2/3/4/5 land + tension_link binding measured + N-21 reaches 6/16 reproductions

f2_unfire_implications_per_framing.F1_A: F2 unfire requires path (a) demote / (b) phi_extractor / (c) substrate redesign (per F1 verdict §F2). Hybrid framing same.
f2_unfire_implications_per_framing.F1_B: F2 cross-substrate broadcast (path d) becomes plausible — tension binding rotates hidden-state covariance. Estimate 15% (per strategic_clm_eeg_akida_tension_2026_05_02 §6).
f2_unfire_implications_per_framing.F1_C: F2 unfire path = (a) + (d). Combined estimate ~25% (paper-only demote arg + tension binding evidence).
